title_en: He Leaked the Secrets of a Southeast Asian Scam Compound. Then He Had to Get Out Alive
title_zh: 他揭露了东南亚一个诈骗“工厂”的秘密，随后必须在生死线上逃离
rubric_en: A source trapped inside an industrial-scale scamming operation contacted me, determined to expose his captors’ crimes—and then escape. This is his story.
rubric_zh: 一位被困在工业化诈骗组织中的消息源联系了我，决心曝光绑架者的罪行，并最终逃出生天。这是他的故事。

paragraphs.en: It was a perfect June evening in New York when I received my first email from the source who would ask me to call him Red Bull. He was writing from hell, 8,000 miles away.
paragraphs.zh: 那是一个完美的纽约六月傍晚，我收到了这位后来要求我称呼他“Red Bull”的消息来源的第一封邮件。那封邮件是从“地狱”里发来的，他距离我足足八千英里。

paragraphs.en: A summer shower had left a rainbow over my Brooklyn neighborhood, and my two children were playing in a kiddie pool on the roof of our apartment building. Now the sun was setting, while I—in typical 21st-century parenting fashion, forgive me—compulsively scrolled through every app on my phone.
paragraphs.zh: 一场夏季阵雨在我布鲁克林的社区上空留下一道彩虹，而我的两个孩子正蹦蹦跳跳地在我们公寓楼顶的儿童小水池里玩耍。此刻，夕阳正在西沉，而我——用惯了二十一世纪的养育方式，请原谅我——不由得在手机上强迫性地刷遍每一个应用。

paragraphs.en: The message had no subject line and came from an address on the encrypted email service Proton Mail: “[email protected].” I opened it.
paragraphs.zh: 这条消息没有主题行，来自加密邮件服务 Proton Mail 的一个地址："[email protected]"。我打开了它。

paragraphs.en: “Hello. I’m currently working inside a major crypto romance scam operation based in the Golden Triangle,” it began. “I am a computer engineer being forced to work here under a contract.”
paragraphs.zh: “你好。我目前在一个位于金三角地区、规模庞大的加密货币爱情诈骗作案团伙中工作。”开头写道。“我是一名计算机工程师，被迫在一份合同下在这里工作。”

paragraphs.en: “I’ve collected internal evidence of how the scam works—step by step,” the message continued. “I am still inside the compound, so I cannot risk direct exposure. But I want to help shut this down.”
paragraphs.zh: “我已经收集了这类诈骗如何运作的内部证据——一步一步的完整链条，”该消息继续写道。“我现在还在园区里面，不能冒险直接露面。但我想帮助把这件事查处。”

paragraphs.en: I knew only vaguely that the Golden Triangle was a lawless jungle region in Southeast Asia. But as a reporter who has covered cryptocurrency crime for the past decade and a half, I understood that crypto scamming—specifically the version of it that’s come to be known as “pig butchering,” in which victims are lured with promises of romance and lucrative investments, only to be tricked into handing over their life savings—has become the most profitable form of cybercrime in the world, pulling in tens of billions of dollars annually.
paragraphs.zh: 我以前只模糊知道“金三角”是东南亚一片法外之地般的荒野区域。但作为一名过去十五年里持续报道加密货币犯罪的记者，我明白，加密货币诈骗——尤其是近年被称为“杀猪盘”的那种骗局，即先用所谓恋爱和高额投资的承诺诱导受害者，最终骗取其全部积蓄——已经成为全球最赚钱的网络犯罪之一，每年可吸金数十亿美元。

paragraphs.en: This sprawling scam industry is, today, staffed by hundreds of thousands of forced laborers in compounds across Myanmar, Cambodia, and Laos. They’re trafficked there from the poorest regions of Asia and Africa and pressed into the service of Chinese organized crime groups. The result is a self-perpetuating, constantly growing, globe-spanning money funnel that destroys lives on both ends—bankrupting one kind of victim, enslaving another.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，这个庞大的诈骗产业在缅甸、柬埔寨和老挝各地的园区里，动员着成千上万名被迫劳动者。人们被从亚洲和非洲最贫困地区拐带进来，随后被迫为中国的有组织犯罪集团服务。结果形成了一个自我循环、持续扩张、横跨全球的资金黑洞，两头都在吞噬生命——一端让受害者破产，另一端让劳动者沦为奴隶。

paragraphs.en: I had read harrowing reports of scam compounds where laborers are beaten, tortured with electric shock batons, starved, and even murdered by their captors. Those stories have mostly come from the rare survivors who have escaped or been rescued by law enforcement. Never before, though, had I heard of someone currently working within a scam compound offering to act as a whistleblower—an actual source on the inside.
paragraphs.zh: 我读过许多惊心动魄的报道，讲述了在诈骗园区里，劳动者遭殴打、电击棍电击、挨饿，甚至被施虐者谋杀的经历。这些故事大多来自极少数逃脱者，或者被执法部门营救出来的人。可从来没有听说过，竟有一个正身处诈骗园区内部的人，主动提出充当告密人——一个真正的内部消息源。

paragraphs.en: I had no idea, still, if this purported source was real. But I wrote back anyway, asking them to switch over from email to the encrypted messaging app Signal and turn on disappearing messages to better cover their tracks.
paragraphs.zh: 我当时仍然不知道这个所谓的消息源是不是真的。但我还是回了信，要求他改用加密通讯应用 Signal，并开启“阅后即焚”功能以更好地掩盖痕迹。

paragraphs.en: The source wrote back immediately and told me to expect to hear from them in two hours.
paragraphs.zh: 消息源马上回复我，并告诉我他们将在两个小时后联系我。

paragraphs.en: That night, after my kids were asleep, the Signal messages began to light up my phone. First, the source sent carefully prepared documents: a flowchart and then a written guide that described the scam processes of the compound in northern Laos. (I’d come to learn that the Golden Triangle—once the American nickname for a vast hotbed of opium and heroin production—now mainly refers to a city-size Laotian “special economic zone” bordering Myanmar and Thailand and largely controlled by Chinese business interests.) The two write-ups described every step in the compound’s work: creating fake Facebook and Instagram profiles; using hired models and AI deepfake tools to complete the illusion of a real romantic prospect; tricking victims into “investing” in fake trading platforms they recommended. It even detailed the small gong that would be struck in the office when someone pulled off a successful scam.
paragraphs.zh: 当晚，在我的孩子们入睡后，Signal 上的消息开始疯狂亮起。首先，消息源发来一份份精心准备的文件：一份流程图，接着是一份文字说明，详细写出了老挝北部那个诈骗园区的作案流程。（我后来得知，“金三角”这个词在今天，已不再主要指代昔日美国人称之为大规模鸦片和海洛因生产温床的区域，如今更常指代一个与缅甸和泰国接壤、由中国商业势力大面积控制的、规模相当于一座城市的老挝“经济特区”。）这两份说明文详细描述了园区工作的每一个环节：创建虚假的 Facebook 和 Instagram 账号；雇佣模特和使用 AI 深度伪造技术，营造出一个真实的浪漫对象；诱导受害者“投资”他们推荐的假交易平台。它甚至写明了当有人成功完成一单诈骗时，办公室里会敲响一只小锣。

paragraphs.en: I had barely had time to skim these detailed descriptions—this was not how I’d planned to spend a Saturday evening with my wife—when my phone rang, just after midnight.
paragraphs.zh: 我几乎还没来得及细读这些详细描述——那晚我本来根本没打算和妻子一起度过一个普通的星期六夜晚——手机就在午夜刚过便响了。

paragraphs.en: I picked up the Signal call. A polite Indian-accented voice said, “Hello.”
paragraphs.zh: 我接起了 Signal 电话。一个带有印度口音却很客气的声音说：“你好。”

paragraphs.en: “What do I call you?” I asked.
paragraphs.zh: “我该怎么称呼你？”我问。

paragraphs.en: “You can call me any name, brother, no matter,” the voice said with a shy laugh.
paragraphs.zh: 那人带着羞怯地笑着说：“兄弟，你想叫我什么都行。”

paragraphs.en: I insisted that I’d need a name for him, even if he just wanted to make one up on the spot.
paragraphs.zh: 我坚持说，即便他只是想当场想个名字，我也需要给他一个称呼。

paragraphs.en: “You can call me Red Bull,” he said. Months later, he would tell me that he’d been looking at an empty can of the energy drink as we spoke.
paragraphs.zh: “你可以叫我 Red Bull，”他说。几个月后，我们通话时他告诉我，他那时一直在盯着一罐空的能量饮料。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull explained that he’d tried reaching out to US and Indian law enforcement agencies and Interpol, as well as the tip lines for a few news outlets, but no one had responded other than me. He asked me to tell him more about myself—and then cut me off as soon as I’d said two sentences about my work covering crypto crime.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 解释说，他曾试图联系美国和印度的执法机构以及国际刑警组织，还向几家新闻机构的爆料热线求助，但除了我之外没有任何人回应。他让我多介绍一下我自己，但我刚说了两句关于自己做加密犯罪报道的经历，他就把我打断了。

paragraphs.en: “So you are the person I share everything with,” he said hurriedly. “And you will help me to expose this, right?”
paragraphs.zh: “所以你就是我可以向你倾诉一切的人，”他急切地说。“你会帮我把这件事曝光，对吗？”

paragraphs.en: Thrown off, I told him he’d have to first tell me who he was.
paragraphs.zh: 我有些怔住，便告诉他他必须先告诉我他是谁。

paragraphs.en: For the next few minutes, Red Bull warily answered my questions. He didn’t tell me his real name but said he was from India—that most of the compound’s forced laborers were from India, Pakistan, or Ethiopia.
paragraphs.zh: 接下来的几分钟里，Red Bull谨慎地回答了我的问题。他没有告诉我真名，但说自己来自印度——化工园里大多数被迫劳动者都来自印度、巴基斯坦或埃塞俄比亚。

paragraphs.en: He was in his early twenties and had a diploma in computer engineering, he said. Like most of his coworkers, Red Bull had been tricked by a fake job offer—in his case, to work as an IT manager for an office in Laos. Then his passport had been taken from him by his Chinese bosses. He was forced to sleep in a dormitory room with five other men and work 15 hours at a time on a nocturnal schedule designed to sync with the daytime of the Indian Americans they targeted for scamming. (That system—matching scammers with victims of their own ethnicity to build rapport and avoid language issues—is common, I’d later learn.)
paragraphs.zh: 他二十出头，据说拥有计算机工程文凭。和大多数同事一样，Red Bull也是被一份假工作邀请骗来的——在他的情况下，是去老挝的一家办公室担任IT经理。随后他的中国老板就夺走了他的护照。他被迫与另外五名男子同住一间宿舍，在夜间班表下连续工作15个小时，作息是为了与目标受害者——印度裔美国人——白天的时间同步。（我后来才知道，这套系统——把诈骗者与同族裔受害者配对，以建立关系并避免语言障碍——很常见。）

paragraphs.en: Red Bull’s situation was not precisely the brutal modern slavery I had read about elsewhere. It was more like a grotesque parody of a corporate sales floor. In theory, the staffers were incentivized with commissions designed to create the illusion that they could get rich from hard work. In reality, they were kept in perpetual debt and servitude. Red Bull told me he was paid a base salary of 3,500 Chinese yuan a month, close to $500, but the money was almost entirely taken from him by daily fines for various infractions, most often not meeting his quota of initial conversations with victims. The result was that he had virtually no income and subsisted off the food in the cafeteria, mostly rice and vegetables that he said tasted of strange chemicals.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull的处境并不完全是我在其他地方读到的那种残酷现代奴役。更像是一场对企业销售大厅的离奇讽刺。按理说，工作人员会通过提成受到激励，制造一种靠辛苦工作就能致富的错觉。现实中，他们却被长期困在债务和奴役之中。Red Bull告诉我，他每月有3500人民币的底薪，接近500美元，但几乎全部都被各种日常罚款扣走，最常见的是未达到与受害者的首次通话指标。结果他几乎没有收入，靠食堂的饭维持生活，主要是米饭和蔬菜，他说味道像奇怪的化学物质。

paragraphs.en: He was bound into this system by a one-year contract, and believed when that time was over—in about six more months—he’d be allowed to leave. So far, he told me, he hadn’t successfully scammed anyone, only skated by with the minimum number of plausible attempts. That meant he was still essentially captive unless he escaped, served out his time, or paid off his contract with thousands of dollars he didn’t have.
paragraphs.zh: 他被一年期合同锁进这个体系，并且他相信当合同期满时——大约还有六个月——就会被允许离开。到目前为止，他告诉我，自己没有成功诈骗任何人，只勉强完成了最少且看似合理的尝试。这意味着，只要不逃跑、服完全部期限，或者拿几千美元把合同“买断”（他并没有这笔钱），他几乎只能一直待在这里。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull told me he’d heard of people who were beaten and electrocuted for breaking rules, a female staffer who he believes was sold into sexual slavery, and other coworkers who mysteriously disappeared. “If they knew I was talking to you or doing something wrong to them, they would directly kill me,” he said. “But I promised myself that whether I stay alive or not, I will stop this scam.”
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 告诉我，他听说过有人因为违背规则而被殴打、电击；一名他认为被卖作性奴的女员工；以及其他神秘失踪的同事。他说：“如果他们知道我在和你说话，或我对他们做了什么不该做的事，他们就会当场杀了我。” “但我对自己下定决心，不管我活着还是死去，我都会把这场诈骗曝光。”

paragraphs.en: Then Red Bull launched into the immediate purpose of his call: There was a scam in progress he knew about, targeting an Indian American man who had already been scammed at least once, but remained in the thrall of one of Red Bull’s colleagues. The man’s crypto wallet service appeared to have frozen his account on the suspicion that he was being defrauded. So for the next payment, a courier was being sent to pick up a six-figure sum in cash.
paragraphs.zh: 随后 Red Bull 直接说到这次来电的核心：他知道一桩正在进行中的诈骗，目标是一位印度裔美国男子，这人已经至少被骗过一次，却仍被 Red Bull 一位同事控制着。那名男子的加密货币钱包服务似乎以他可能被骗为由冻结了他的账户。于是，在下一笔付款时，会派一名快递员去取一笔六位数现金。

paragraphs.en: The pickup was set to happen in three or four days. The victim lived just a few hours away from me. If I acted quickly, Red Bull explained, I could alert law enforcement and help set up a sting operation to capture the courier. Beyond this tip, he wanted me to find him an FBI agent to be his handler going forward, while continuing to work with me as a source. We’d spoken for just over 10 minutes.
paragraphs.zh: 接货将安排在三到四天后。受害人离我只有几个小时的车程。如果我行动迅速，Red Bull 解释说，我可以通知执法机关，并协助安排一次执法钓鱼抓捕，逮捕那名快递员。除了这个线索，他还希望我帮他找一名 FBI 探员，今后由其做联络人，并让他继续作为线人配合我。我们刚聊了十多分钟。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull impatiently said that he’d write me the details on Signal and hung up. Within seconds, he was sending screenshots of the compound’s internal chat logs, his colleagues’ conversations with victims, and more details on the sting operation he wanted me to arrange.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 有些不耐烦地说他会在 Signal 上把细节发给我，然后就挂了电话。几秒钟后，他就开始发送该园区内部聊天记录、他同事与受害者的对话截图，以及更多关于他想让我安排的这次抓捕行动的细节。

paragraphs.en: With my mind reeling, I paused for a moment. Then I called Red Bull back on Signal without warning—with video enabled. I wanted to see who I was talking to.
paragraphs.zh: 我的脑子一片混乱，停顿了一下。随后我毫无预警地通过 Signal 回拨给 Red Bull，并开启了视频。我想看清我正在和谁说话。

paragraphs.en: The view from the hotel room where Red Bull first spoke to WIRED, as seen over a Signal video call.
paragraphs.zh: 这是 Red Bull 首次通过 Signal 视频通话向 WIRED 讲述时所在酒店房间的画面。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull picked up. He was slim and handsome, with slightly shaggy hair and a trim beard. He gave me a half smile, seemingly unconcerned about revealing his face. I asked him to show me his surroundings, and he flipped the video to display a bare hotel room—he explained that he’d risked booking a room in the hotel next to his office to have somewhere to talk to me—and the view out the window of ugly concrete buildings and parking lots, construction sites, and a few palm trees. At my request, he walked outside and showed me the Chinese-language sign on the front of the building. I didn’t know much about the Golden Triangle, but this appeared to be it.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 接起了电话。他身材瘦削、相貌英俊，头发有些蓬松，胡子修剪得很整洁。他对我露出一个半笑，似乎对露出自己的脸并不在意。我请他给我看一下他身边的环境，他把画面转向，展示了一个空荡荡的酒店房间——他说为了有地方和我通话，他冒着风险在办公室旁边订了这间房——以及窗外丑陋的混凝土建筑、停车场、工地和几棵椰子树。我要求后，他走到外面，给我看了建筑物正门上的中文牌匾。我对金三角并不了解，但这看起来正是那里。

paragraphs.en: Finally, Red Bull showed me his work ID, with a Chinese name the compound had given him: Machao. (None of the workers in the office, he explained, knew each other’s real name.)
paragraphs.zh: 最后，Red Bull 给我看了他的工牌，上面写着一个中文名字——这个化名是他在园区内被取的：Machao。 （他解释说，办公室里的工人都不知道彼此的真名。）

paragraphs.en: I began to believe Red Bull was what he said: a real whistleblower in a Laos scam compound. I told him I’d consider everything he’d asked for, but that I wanted to work with him patiently and carefully to minimize his risk.
paragraphs.zh: 我开始相信“Red Bull”所说的话：他真的是一名老挝骗局窝点中的举报人。他告诉我，我可以考虑他提出的所有要求，但前提是我希望我们能耐心、谨慎地合作，以尽量降低他的风险。

paragraphs.en: “I am with you and whatever I do, I do under your guidance,” he wrote back at 1:33 am. “Have a nice night ahead.”
paragraphs.zh: “不管我做什么，都听您的指示。”他在凌晨1:33回道。“祝您晚安。”

paragraphs.en: At 4 am, I was still lying awake in bed, considering what to do with the eager new source who seemed determined to put his life in my hands.
paragraphs.zh: 凌晨4点，我仍躺在床上辗转反侧，思考该如何处理这位满怀热忱、似乎已决心将生死托付于我的新消息源。

paragraphs.en: After a few hours’ sleep, I texted Erin West, a California prosecutor—or, as I would learn in our phone call later that day, a former prosecutor. Toward the end of 2024, West became so fed up with the US government’s failure to do anything about the pig butchering epidemic that she’d retired early from her position as a deputy district attorney and was now focused full-time on running her own anti-scam organization, called Operation Shamrock.
paragraphs.zh: 睡了几个小时后，我给加利福尼亚州检察官Erin West发了短信——后来当天的电话里我才得知，她其实是前检察官。到2024年底，West对美国政府对“杀猪盘”疫情般泛滥的冷漠感到彻底失望，于是她提前从副检察官职位提前退休，如今全职运营自己的反诈骗机构，名叫“邪恶草莓行动（Operation Shamrock）”。

paragraphs.en: I asked West for advice about who in law enforcement could help arrange the sting that Red Bull had requested. West, to my surprise, was far more excited about the story that Red Bull wanted me to write. “This is a major, major deal,” West said. “Here’s someone on the inside who’s willing to share this information and tell us everything about how this whole operation runs.”
paragraphs.zh: 我向韦斯特咨询，问她是否有执法部门的人可以帮忙安排“捞捕”行动，这是红牛想让我去做的。令我惊讶的是，韦斯特对红牛想让我写的这条消息异常兴奋。

paragraphs.en: But she quickly shot down the idea of a sting. No time to arrange it, she said. Nor did she think that arresting a lowly money mule would be the major win that Red Bull believed it to be. Most couriers of this kind, she said, were freelancers even further down the hierarchy of a scam operation than Red Bull himself and would know nothing of value.
paragraphs.zh: “这是个大消息，大新闻。”韦斯特说，“里面有人愿意站出来提供消息，告诉我们这个骗局从头到尾是怎么运作的。”

paragraphs.en: More important, a sting—or any effort to warn the victim myself by asking Red Bull for his contact and reaching out—could also create suspicion of a leak inside the scam compound that might be traced to Red Bull and put his life in jeopardy. Preventing one case of fraud, or taking down one courier, hardly seemed worth that risk of exposure.
paragraphs.zh: 但她很快否定了安排捞捕的想法。她说，根本来不及组织。她也不认为抓捕一个地位很低的“钱驮子”会是红牛所认为的那种重大胜利。她说，这类快递员通常是自由职业者，在骗局的金字塔里层级甚至比红牛本人还要低得多，几乎什么有价值的信息都不知道。

paragraphs.en: I had been talking to Red Bull for less than 24 hours. Already I was making the decision to stand by as a potential six-figure scam took place, as a cost of protecting him.
paragraphs.zh: 更重要的是，做一次捞捕，或者任何我自己去报警、先向红牛要到联系人再直接联系受害者的做法，都可能暴露骗局内部有人泄密，而且这种泄密很可能被追溯到红牛身上，危及他的生命。为了防止他暴露而冒着风险，只为了阻止一例诈骗，或者只抓到一名快递员，这样的风险几乎不值得。

paragraphs.en: Beyond this question of a sting, West told me, she wasn’t sure that handing Red Bull over to the FBI was the right approach. If he were to become a law enforcement source, she said, the FBI or Interpol would almost certainly tell him to stop talking to me or any other journalist. And the result of whatever he shared with the feds would likely fall far short of his expectations: criminal charges, in absentia, for low-level bosses at best. “If he thinks that the FBI and Interpol are going to march into Laos and take this place down, that’s never going to happen. The cavalry is not coming.”
paragraphs.zh: West告诉我，除了如何反捕这一点，她并不确定把“红牛”交给FBI是否是正确做法。她说，如果他成为执法部门的线人，FBI或国际刑警组织几乎肯定会要求他停止与我或任何其他记者联系。无论他向联邦机构透露什么，其结果恐怕会远远低于他的预期：充其量只会对一些低层头目提出缺席起诉。“如果他以为FBI和国际刑警会进军老挝把这个地方端掉，那永远不会发生。救援军不会到来。”

paragraphs.en: Far more valuable than building a case against this single operation, she argued, would be to use whatever Red Bull could share to tell the larger story: the high-resolution reality of pig butchering compounds, their operational details, the scale of their work. Some of this had been described before by survivors of the compounds, but never, to West’s knowledge, by an insider source leaking documents and evidence in real time.
paragraphs.zh: 她认为，与其围绕单一案件建立起诉，更有价值的是利用红牛能提供的信息，讲清更大的故事：对“杀猪盘”作坊的高还原现实、它们的运作细节、以及其业务规模。此前确实有幸存者讲过作坊的情况，但据West所知，从未有过像他这样由内部来源实时泄露文件和证据的人。

paragraphs.en: The role of human trafficking in scam compounds’ operations has only become harder to measure, West told me, with the Trump administration’s destruction of USAID, which funded humanitarian organizations in the region. “The Trump presidency has taken away any of the eyes on the ground that we had,” West said.
paragraphs.zh: West告诉我，特朗普政府解散USAID以来，诈骗作坊运作中贩运人口这一环节更难被量化。USAID曾为该地区的人道组织提供资金。West说：“特朗普总统任期把我们在当地原本拥有的‘一线观察点’全部拿走了。”

paragraphs.en: All of that has enabled Chinese-origin gangs to continue stealing “a generation’s worth of our wealth” through a system of slavery that’s increasingly come to control an entire region of the world, as West described it. “The story here is how we allowed these criminals to embed themselves in Southeast Asia like a festering cancer,” West said. “And how it’s disrupting our ability to trust people.”
paragraphs.zh: 所有这些都让源自中国的犯罪团伙得以继续通过一种日益蔓延、控制着一个世界区域的奴役体系，窃取“整整一代人的财富”，正如West所说。“这个故事在于我们如何让这些罪犯像顽固肿瘤一样在东南亚扎根，”West说，“以及这如何正在瓦解我们对他人的信任。”

paragraphs.en: I told Red Bull that we couldn’t arrange a sting operation without risking his life. I also made the case that if he wanted to be a source for me, he’d likely have to wait to talk to law enforcement. He accepted all this with surprising decisiveness. “OK, done,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 我告诉红牛，如果不冒着他生命危险，我们就不能安排诱捕行动。我还向他说了一个道理：如果他想成为我的线人，他很可能必须等到有机会再与执法部门接触。他非常果断地接受了我的全部判断。“好了，决定了。”他说。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull and I soon settled into a routine of speaking on Signal every morning New York time, around 10 pm in Laos, as he walked around outside his dormitory in the half hour after he woke up and before he went into the cafeteria for a meal. (This “dinner” preceded his approximately 15-hour workday, which included two breaks to eat.)
paragraphs.zh: 红牛和我很快形成了一个固定模式，每天纽约时间早上（也就是他在老挝当地大约晚上10点）通话一次，在他起床后和去食堂吃饭前的半小时里，我通常通过Signal和他联系。（这顿“晚餐”在他大约15小时的工作日之前，而这个工作日包含两次进食休息。）

paragraphs.en: He spent much of our first conversations pitching a series of increasingly high-risk evidence-gathering methods: He wanted to wear a hidden camera or microphone. He suggested setting up remote desktop software so I could see in real time everything on his screen. He offered to install spyware on the computer of his team leader, another Indian worker with aviator glasses and a short beard who went by the name “Amani.” He even proposed to hack into the laptop of Amani’s supervisor, 50k, a short Chinese man with a paunch, tight pants, and a tattoo on his chest that Red Bull could never quite make out. Maybe that spyware would help us gather intel on 50k’s communications with his own boss, “Alang,” whom Red Bull never saw in person.
paragraphs.zh: 我们最初的许多次谈话里，他都在提出一连串风险越来越高的取证方案：他想戴上隐藏摄像头或麦克风。他提议我在他的屏幕上远程安装桌面软件，这样我就能实时看到他电脑上的一切。他还主动提出在他的组长电脑上安装间谍软件；这名组长是另一位印度同事，戴飞行员眼镜、留短胡子，名字叫“阿曼尼”。他甚至提出入侵组长上司“50k”那台笔记本电脑，50k是一名中国小个子男，肚子有些发福，穿着紧身裤，胸前有一处红牛始终看不清的纹身。也许有了这种间谍软件，我们就能掌握50k与自己的上司“阿朗”通信的情报，而红牛从未见过阿朗本人。

paragraphs.en: For every one of these brazen ideas, I consulted with colleagues and experts who told me, one after another, how hidden-camera evidence-gathering required training, how the software Red Bull wanted to install on the office computers left behind detectable artifacts—why, in other words, all these ideas were likely to get him caught and killed.
paragraphs.zh: 对于他这些每一个明目张胆的想法，我都向同事和专家请教。每个人都告诉我：用隐藏摄像头取证需要经过专业训练；红牛想在办公室电脑上安装的软件会留下可被检测到的痕迹——换句话说，这些点子很可能会让他暴露，最终丢掉命。

paragraphs.en: We settled on a far simpler approach: He would use Signal on his work computer to send me messages and materials throughout his work hours, using Signal’s disappearing messages feature set to a five-minute countdown timer to cover his tracks. At times he started calling me “Uncle” to bolster the cover story that he was just speaking to a relative, in case he was caught.
paragraphs.zh: 我们最后采用了一种更简单的方式：他会在工作时间用工作电脑上的Signal给我发消息和资料，并把Signal的阅后即焚功能设置为五分钟倒计时，以掩盖自己的行踪。有时他会开始叫我“叔叔”，以加强这个掩护故事——他只是和一位亲属聊天，以防他被当场抓住。

paragraphs.en: We adopted a protocol in which one of us would start a conversation by saying “Red.” The other would respond “Bull.” This exchange would verify no one had taken over his account. It was Red Bull’s idea to change the name and icon of the Signal app on his computer to make it look like a desktop shortcut for its hard drive.
paragraphs.zh: 我们制定了一个流程：我们中的任何一方先发一句“Red”，对方再回“Bull”。这次问答可以确认没有人接管了他的账号。之所以把Signal应用和图标改名改成硬盘桌面快捷方式的样子，正是Red Bull的主意。

paragraphs.en: He began to send me a steady stream of pictures, screenshots, and videos: a spreadsheet and photos of a whiteboard on which his team’s work was tracked, with scam totals in the thousands of dollars next to many of the group’s nicknames. A photo of a Chinese ceremonial drum on a stand that stood in the office, ready to be struck to celebrate big wins of $100,000 or more. Pages and pages of chat logs, posted to the office WhatsApp group, documented the scamming wins of Red Bull’s colleagues and the tragic responses from victims: “Always had a dream of having a girlfriend then wife like you” … “U stopped talking to me” … “I will continue praying for your Mom” … “Please help me withdraw my money OK?” … “??????” … “😭” One video showed a victim crying in his car after losing a six-figure sum; the mark had sent the clip to his scammer to elicit guilt, perhaps, but it was instead being passed around the office for laughs.
paragraphs.zh: 他开始给我持续发送照片、截图和视频：有一份电子表格和一块白板的照片，白板上记录着他所在小组的工作进度，许多组员的代号旁边写着以千美元计的诈骗总额。一张办公室里支在支架上的中国礼仪鼓的照片，似乎随时可以敲响，以庆祝十万美元以上的巨大收益。成页的聊天记录被上传到办公室的WhatsApp群，记录了Red Bull同事的诈骗成功和受害者悲惨回应：\“一直梦想有个像你这样的女朋友，后来是妻子\”……\“你不再和我说话了\”……\“我会继续为你妈妈祈祷\”……\“求你帮我把钱提现出去，行吗\”……\“??????\”……\“😭\”有一段视频里，一个受害者在车里哭泣，在亏掉一笔六位数金额后。受害者把这段视频发给骗子，似乎是为了激起内疚感，结果却反而在办公室被当成笑料传阅。

paragraphs.en: Every member of the team was required to post daily updates—how many “first chats” they’d started, how many “deep chats” they’d had, the kind that might lead to successful scams. Their group chat used euphemisms like “opening a new customer,” for hooking a new mark, and “recharges,” for repeat victims. Each team had a quota, typically around a million dollars a month. If they met it, they’d be rewarded with weekends off, the freedom to have snacks in the office, even parties at a nearby club. (Their bosses, Red Bull said, would spend those parties in a private room separated by a curtain.) Miss the target, and they’d be berated, fined, and forced to work seven nights a week.
paragraphs.zh: 团队每个成员都必须每日更新：他们开启了多少“首次对话”、有多少“深度对话”，也就是可能导致成功诈骗的对话。群聊里还会用“开新客户”来代称勾住新受害者，用“充值”来代称重复作案的受害者。每个团队都有一个配额，通常每月大约一百万美元。完成配额后，他们会得到奖励，比如周末休息、办公室内可随意吃零食，甚至还能去附近夜总会聚会（Red Bull说，他的上司们会在一个有隔帘分开的包厢里开那种聚会）。若没达标，他们就会被斥责、罚款，并被迫每周工作七晚。

paragraphs.en: A whiteboard in the office tracked scam successes, listed alongside workers’ pseudonyms and team names.
paragraphs.zh: 办公室里的一块白板记录着诈骗成功案例，并列出了员工的化名和团队名称。

paragraphs.en: Every worker also posted a mandatory daily schedule—not of their own nocturnal life sitting at a desk in a fluorescent-lit office and sending Facebook and Instagram messages, but the schedule of the rich, single woman they were pretending to be: 7 am “peaceful yoga and meditation,” 9:30 am “self-care and vacation planning,” 2:30 pm “dentist visit,” 6 pm “dinner and talking with mother.”
paragraphs.zh: 每位员工还必须张贴一份每日行程，并不是他们自己在荧光灯照亮的办公室里、熬夜坐在桌前发送 Facebook 和 Instagram 信息的真实生活，而是他们假扮的那位富有未婚女性的行程表：7 点“平静的瑜伽与冥想”，9:30 “自我保养与度假计划”，2:30 “看牙医”，6 点“晚餐并与母亲通话”。

paragraphs.en: Sometimes during our voice calls, Red Bull would tell me to enable video and record my screen. Then he would walk into the cafeteria and surreptitiously film his surroundings while pretending to talk to his “uncle.” I got a tour through the bright lights of the building’s lobby and stairwells, the lines of depressed-looking South Asian and African men lining up for food. Once he even showed me the inside of the office, a large, beige room where I could see clusters of desks with red, yellow, and green flags on them that connoted each team’s scamming performance.
paragraphs.zh: 有时在我们的语音通话中，Red Bull 会让我打开视频并录制屏幕。接着他会走进自助餐厅，在“和他的‘叔叔’通话”之下偷偷拍摄周围环境。我因此看到了大楼大厅和楼梯间明亮的灯光，看到一排排神情沮丧的南亚和非洲男子排队取餐。有一次他甚至让我看到办公室内部，那是一个大而米色的房间，我能看到一排排桌子，上面挂着红、黄、绿三色旗帜，代表各个团队的诈骗业绩。

paragraphs.en: A video surreptitiously recorded via a Signal call shows the inside of the Boshang scam compound’s office.
paragraphs.zh: 一段通过 Signal 通话悄悄录制的视频展示了博商诈骗园区办公室的内部情况。

paragraphs.en: After a few days, Red Bull and I tried upgrading our cover story, and I became a secret girlfriend he was texting with—a better explanation for his use of Signal should it be detected. We peppered our conversation with heart emojis, referred to each other as “dear,” and signed off with “miss you,” until our chat logs started to look almost like the fraud romances his team carried on daily. But we soon found the pretense too embarrassing and gave it up.
paragraphs.zh: 几天后，红牛和我开始升级我们编造的身份，他让我成了他“秘密谈恋爱”的对象，我会在手机上给他发消息，以便如果对方发现异常，也能更好地解释他为何使用 Signal。我们在聊天里加了不少爱心表情，彼此叫对方“亲爱的”，又常常以“想你了”收尾，结果聊天记录很快看起来几乎和他的团队每天进行的诈骗甜言蜜语没什么区别。但很快我们都觉得这番伪装太尴尬，于是放弃了。

paragraphs.en: On another occasion, as I was heading to sleep, Red Bull wrote a surprisingly sensitive farewell message: “Good night! 🌙 Rest easy—you’ve done enough for today. Let your mind reset, and let tomorrow come with fresh clarity and quiet strength.”
paragraphs.zh: 有一次，当我正准备睡觉时，红牛发来了一条出乎意料温和而细腻的告别信息：“晚安！🌙 放轻松，你今天做得已经够了。让大脑先重置，明天带着新的清醒和静静的力量迎接新的一天。”

paragraphs.en: As stilted as the language felt, I admit to being moved by the unusually thoughtful note—I had, in fact, gotten very little sleep over the several stressful days since we’d first started communicating.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管这些话听起来有些生硬，我不得不承认这段特别体贴的留言让我很受触动——事实上，自从我们开始联系以来，过去几天我几乎没怎么好好睡过。

paragraphs.en: Then, during our call the following morning, Red Bull began explaining to me the role that AI chat tools like ChatGPT and DeepSeek play in the compound’s work: how they’re trained to use them to clean up their language, find just the right sentiment, never run out of inviting turns of phrase.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早上的通话中，红牛开始向我解释 AI 聊天工具（比如 ChatGPT 和 DeepSeek）在该园区作业中的作用：他们如何训练自己去用这些工具把话术打磨得更顺耳，精准找到合适的情绪基调，并且从不缺少能让对方心动的表达。

paragraphs.en: His goodnight message the previous evening, he told me without hesitation, had been copied directly from ChatGPT. “Everyone does this here; they teach us this,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 前一晚的晚安信息，他毫不犹豫地告诉我，是直接从 ChatGPT 复制的。“这里每个人都这么做；他们就是这样教我们的，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Funny, I thought, how easy it is to be taken in by a bit of sympathetic text sent by a new acquaintance on the other side of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 我心想，真奇怪，来自地球另一端的新认识者发来几句看似同情的话，竟然这么容易让人上当受骗。

paragraphs.en: In the few minutes I had with Red Bull every day between the dormitory and the office, amid our other conversations about his safety and evidence-gathering tactics, I asked how he’d come to be trapped in the compound and why he had become so singularly motivated to expose it. In answering, in hurried snippets of conversation and then later in longer texts, he told me the 23-year story of his life.
paragraphs.zh: 在我每天在宿舍和办公室之间与Red Bull短短见面交流的几分钟里，除了一起讨论他的安全和取证策略，我们还聊了他为何陷入这个园区，以及为什么他会异常坚定地想把它曝光。回答我的时候，他一会儿急切地说几句，一会儿后来又用更长的信息告诉我，他讲述了自己23年的一生。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull grew up, he told me, as one of eight children in a Muslim family in a mountainous village in Jammu and Kashmir, a disputed territory on the India–Pakistan border. His father was a schoolteacher but also sometimes worked as a construction laborer and, along with Red Bull’s mother, raised dairy cattle and sold ghee—clarified butter—to survive.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull告诉我，他在印度与巴基斯坦边界上的一个有争议地区、查谟和克什米尔的一个山区村庄里，作为穆斯林家庭八个孩子中的一个长大。父亲是个学校教师，但有时也做建筑工人，后来他和Red Bull的母亲一起养奶牛、出售酥油（澄清奶油）来维持生计。

paragraphs.en: When Red Bull was a young child in the mid-2000s, the family would often leave their village for areas in northern Kashmir to escape the intermittent conflicts between the Indian Army and Pakistani-supported guerrillas. Muslim men in the region had sometimes been conscripted to fight or carry supplies for Pakistani-backed forces, then branded as terrorists and killed by India’s military.
paragraphs.zh: 2000年代中期红牛还只是个小孩时，家人常常带他离开村庄，前往北克什米尔的地区躲避印度军队与巴基斯坦支持的游击队之间断断续续的冲突。当地的一些穆斯林男性曾被征召上前作战或给巴基斯坦支持的武装力量运送补给，之后又被印度军方当作“恐怖分子”加以处决。

paragraphs.en: When the conflict died down, Red Bull’s parents sent him to live with his grandparents in the city of Rajouri, a four-hour bus ride away, where they hoped their unusually bright and inquisitive child could get a better education. His grandparents were harsh guardians, he told me. They forced him to chop wood and fetch water when he wasn’t studying, and his school was a 6-mile walk away. He wore out his shoes, blistered his feet, and attended classes with a rope tied around his pants for a belt.
paragraphs.zh: 冲突平息后，红牛的父母把他送到离家约四小时巴士路程的拉杰奥里城，寄居在祖父母家，希望这个异常聪慧、好奇心强的孩子能接受更好的教育。他告诉我，祖父母是严厉的监护人。每当他没在学习时，就被迫砍柴和挑水；而他的学校离家有6英里远。他把鞋子磨坏、脚起了水泡，还要系着一根当作皮带用的绳子去上课。

paragraphs.en: Even then, he says, he maintained a kind of defiant optimism. “I kept thinking: If not today then tomorrow things will get better,” he wrote to me.
paragraphs.zh: 即便在那时，他说自己也始终保持着一种反叛式的乐观。“我一直这样想：今天不行，那就明天会更好，”他写信告诉我。

paragraphs.en: When he was 15, Red Bull’s grandparents sent him to live with the family of a pair of his teachers, who made him work as a servant in exchange for paying his school fees. He would wake up early every morning to clean the house before breakfast, then wash the dishes before going to school—including the separate set they required him to use.
paragraphs.zh: 15岁时，红牛的祖父母又把他送到一对老师的家庭中同住。那对老师同意支付他的学费，但以让他当佣人来交换。他每天清晨起得很早，在上饭前先打扫房屋，然后洗碗，最后再去学校——连他必须使用的一套单独餐具也由他清理。

paragraphs.en: One day in that house, he remembers watching, entranced, as the family’s eldest son played the latest FIFA game on his PC, the first time Red Bull had ever seen a computer. He was told to get back to work. This became the beginning of his computer fixation. “I felt ashamed and disrespected because I was not even allowed to touch it,” Red Bull wrote. “I told myself that one day I would become the master of this machine.”
paragraphs.zh: 在那栋房子里的一天，他记得自己看得入了迷：家中长子在电脑上玩最新的 FIFA 游戏，而那是 Red Bull 有生以来第一次看到电脑。结果他被叫去继续做活——这成了他沉迷电脑的开始。“我感到羞耻和被冒犯，因为我甚至不被允许碰它。”Red Bull 写道。“我在心里下定决心，总有一天我会成为这台机器的主人。”

paragraphs.en: After a particularly humiliating scolding, Red Bull decided to run away. He left the next morning before the family was awake and traveled to the city, where he found odd jobs cleaning houses, doing construction work, cutting rice. For a time he went door-to-door selling Ayurvedic medicines. At night he would study alone in the room he rented. In 2021, he was accepted into the computer science program at Kashmir Government Polytechnic College in Srinagar, the region’s biggest city.
paragraphs.zh: 在一次尤其羞辱性的训斥后，Red Bull 决定逃跑。他第二天清晨在一家人醒来前离开，去了城市，在那里干过杂活：打扫房屋、做建筑活、剁米饭。曾有一段时间，他挨家挨户推销阿育吠陀草药。晚上，他就在自己租的房间里独自学习。2021 年，他被录取到查谟和克什米尔首府斯利那加的查谟和克什米尔政府理工学院计算机科学专业。

paragraphs.en: At the university, he slept in a room without proper bedding through the freezing Kashmiri winters, and often went hungry. A friend taught him how to make Facebook pages for businesses, or buy and sell them like a real estate developer flipping properties. Working on the school’s PCs, he soon made the equivalent of $200, enough to buy his own used Dell laptop—a prized, life-changing possession.
paragraphs.zh: 在大学里，他在没有合适床铺的房间里度过漫长而寒冷的克什米尔冬天，常常挨饿。一个朋友教他为企业做 Facebook 页面，或者像房地产开发商倒卖房产那样买进再卖掉。借着在学校电脑上练习，他很快赚到了相当于 200 美元的收入，足够买下一台二手的戴尔笔记本电脑——一件珍贵且改变人生的财产。

paragraphs.en: After three years of studying, working, and sending money home to his family, he graduated with a diploma in computer engineering—the first time, he says, that anyone from his village had ever attained that level of technical education. He’d also developed a stubborn, even angry determination to chart his own path through the world.
paragraphs.zh: 经过三年学习、打工和寄钱回家，他以计算机工程文凭毕业，这在他口中是他所在村子里从未有人做到过的技术教育高度。他还形成了一种固执，甚至带着愤怒的决心，要为自己的人生自己划出一条路。

paragraphs.en: “My mom and dad always advised me to have patience and to stay strong, and their advice gave me some inner strength, but the fight itself I always carried alone,” he wrote. “It is very hard for anyone to truly understand me, but I never stopped fighting my circumstances.”
paragraphs.zh: “我爸妈总是劝我要有耐心、要坚强，他们的劝告给了我一点内在的力量，但真正的搏斗一直是我一个人扛着。没人能真正理解我，但我从未停止反抗自己的处境。”

paragraphs.en: Not long after Red Bull graduated, he was making a livable wage creating Facebook pages and websites, earning as much as a thousand dollars a month. But he had bigger ambitions. He dreamed of working in artificial intelligence, in the biomedical field, or in cybersecurity as a whitehat hacker. (The TV show Mr. Robot had long been one of his favorites.) He wanted to study abroad but couldn’t afford it, and was rejected when he sought student loans.
paragraphs.zh: 红牛（Red Bull）毕业不久后，靠制作 Facebook 页面和网站谋生，月入可达一千美元，能勉强维持生活。但他有更大的抱负。他梦想着从事人工智能、 生物医药，或成为一名白帽黑客从事网络安全工作。（《黑客军团》这部电视剧一直是他最喜欢的剧集。）他想出国读书，却负担不起，申请学生贷款也遭到拒绝。

paragraphs.en: He resigned himself to working for a year or two to save money. A friend from college told him of someone in Laos who seemed to be able to find people good work. Red Bull began talking to that thirdhand contact, called Ajaz, who said he knew an agent who could get him hired as an office IT manager making around $1,700 a month. For Red Bull, that alluring salary would mean he might only have to work for a single year before returning to school.
paragraphs.zh: 他打算先工作一两年存钱。一个大学同学介绍他认识一个在老挝的中间人，据说能帮人找到不错的工作。红牛开始和那个三手联系的人阿贾兹（Ajaz）谈起职业事宜，阿贾兹说他认识一位中介，可以帮他找到一份大约每月1,700美元的办公室 IT 经理工作。对红牛来说，这样有吸引力的薪水意味着他也许只需工作一年，就能再回去上学。

paragraphs.en: Ajaz told Red Bull to fly to Bangkok and then call the recruiting agent from the airport. He boarded the plane without even knowing what industry his employer might be in—only that he would help manage its computers. He remembers the excitement of traveling abroad for the first time, dreaming of his future throughout the red-eye flight across the Indian Ocean.
paragraphs.zh: 阿贾兹让红牛先飞去曼谷，再在机场给那位招聘中介打电话。他登上飞机时甚至不知道自己未来的雇主可能在哪个行业——只知道自己会去帮忙管理公司的电脑。那是他第一次出国，他记得在跨越印度洋的红眼航班上一路兴奋不已，心里全是对未来的幻想。

paragraphs.en: The next morning in Bangkok, he called the agent, an East African man who summarily told him to take a 12-hour bus ride to Chiang Mai, and then a taxi to the border with Laos. When Red Bull arrived there, he was to take a selfie showing that he was outside the immigration office, and text it to the agent. A few minutes after Red Bull did as instructed, an immigration official came outside, flashed the selfie he’d evidently received from the agent, and demanded 500 Thai baht—about $15. Red Bull paid, the official stamped his passport, and he was sent down to a boat waiting on the Mekong River below. The ferry crossed the river just south of the point where the three borders of Thailand, Laos, and Myanmar meet in a single nexus: the Golden Triangle.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早上在曼谷，他给那名中介打了电话。对方是个东非男子，直接命令他说：先坐12小时巴士到清迈，再打车前往老挝边境。在红牛抵达后，他要在移民局外面拍一张自拍，并把照片发给中介。红牛照做后没过几分钟，一名移民官员就出来了，拿着他显然已从中介那里收到的那张自拍，要求他支付500泰铢（约15美元）。红牛照付了款，官员在护照上盖了章，然后把他送到湄公河下游一艘在等他的船上。渡船在河上穿过一段，正好在泰国、老挝和缅甸三国边界汇合于一点的南侧：金三角地带。

paragraphs.en: After the boat had crossed into Laos, a young Chinese man waiting on the opposite river bank showed Red Bull the same selfie. He took Red Bull’s passport without explanation and gave it to immigration officials along with some Chinese currency. It came back with a visa.
paragraphs.zh: 船只进入老挝后，一名年轻的中国男子在对岸河岸等着他，拿出同样的自拍照给红牛看。那人没有解释，直接拿走了红牛的护照，连同一些人民币交给移民官员处理，护照很快便换回了一张签证。

paragraphs.en: The Chinese man pocketed the passport and told Red Bull to wait for the East African agent. Then he left, taking Red Bull’s passport with him.
paragraphs.zh: 那名中国男子把护照塞进口袋，告诉红牛等东非中介过来。随后便离开了，并把红牛的护照带走。

paragraphs.en: An hour later, the agent arrived and drove him in a white van to a hotel in northern Laos, where he would spend the night. Lying in the bed of that bare hotel room, he remained entirely focused on the anxiety and excitement of his first real job interview, scheduled for the next day. He still suspected nothing.
paragraphs.zh: 一个小时后，中介赶来，把他从清晨坐了一天白天的行程后带上了一辆白色面包车，去往老挝北部的一家酒店，在那里过夜。躺在那间空荡荡酒店房间的床上，他整晚都只想着次日的第一场真正求职面试，既紧张又兴奋。他仍然没有察觉到任何端倪。

paragraphs.en: The next morning, he was brought to an office, a gray tower of concrete surrounded by other drab buildings amid the lush green mountains of northern Laos. Red Bull sat nervously at a desk as a Chinese man and a translator administered a typing test and an English language test, both of which he breezed through. They told him he’d passed, and they began asking him about his familiarity with social networks like Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早上，他们把他带到一间办公室，这是一座灰色的混凝土塔楼，周围是寂淡的建筑群，坐落在老挝北部苍翠的群山之间。中方“红牛”坐在桌前，神情紧张，一名中国男子和一名翻译为他进行打字测试和英语测试，他都轻松通过。他们告诉他，测试合格，并开始问他对 Facebook、Instagram 和 LinkedIn 等社交网络的熟悉程度。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull eagerly answered their questions. Finally they asked him whether he understood the job he’d be starting. “As an IT manager?” he asked. No, they said, for once speaking without euphemism: He would be a “scammer.”
paragraphs.zh: “红牛”急切地回答了他们的问题。最后，他们问他是否明白自己将要开始的工作是什么。“我会是个 IT 经理吗？”他问。对方说不是，他们这次说得直白些：他将成为一名“骗子”。

paragraphs.en: As the reality of his situation finally became clear, Red Bull spiraled into panic. The Chinese boss told him he’d be starting immediately. Trying to buy time, he begged to instead return to the hotel to rest for one night before beginning work. The boss agreed.
paragraphs.zh: 当他终于意识到自己处境的真相时，“红牛”陷入了惊慌。中国老板告诉他，他必须立刻上岗。为了争取时间，他恳求能先回酒店休息一晚再开始工作。老板同意了。

paragraphs.en: That night in the hotel room, Red Bull frantically searched the internet for information about scam operations in the Golden Triangle. Only then did he see the dimensions of the trap that had sprung around him: Too late, he read about the thousands of Indians deceived and ensnared just as he had been, with no passport or means of escape. In the midst of this sickening epiphany, his parents video-called him to ask if he’d gotten the IT manager job. Burying his shame and regret, he said he had, smiled, and accepted their congratulations.
paragraphs.zh: 那晚在酒店房间里，“红牛”疯狂地在网上搜索有关“金三角”地区诈骗团伙的资料。直到那时，他才意识到自己已陷入的陷阱有多大：太晚了，他读到成千上万名印度人像他一样被欺骗并陷进去，既没有护照，也没有任何逃脱的手段。就在这令人作呕的顿悟中，父母给他打来视频电话，问他是否拿到 IT 经理的工作。他把羞愧和悔恨都压下去，谎称拿到了，笑着接受了他们的祝贺。

paragraphs.en: The colored flags in each team’s work area indicate whether it’s been meeting scam revenue quotas.
paragraphs.zh: 每个小组工作区里都有彩色旗帜，显示该组是否完成了诈骗营收配额。

paragraphs.en: A Chinese ceremonial drum stands ready to be struck by any worker who achieves a scam of $100,000 or more.
paragraphs.zh: 一面中国礼仪鼓随时可以被任何完成十万美元及以上诈骗的员工敲打。

paragraphs.en: Over the next days, with little in the way of orientation, he was pulled into the machinery of the scamming organization he’d come to know as the Boshang compound: He was trained to create fake profiles, given scam scripts, and then set to work on a nocturnal schedule, manually spamming out hundreds of introductory messages every night to lure new victims. At the end of his shifts, he would return to the top bunk of his six-man dorm room—little bigger than the hotel room he’d occupied those first nights—with a toilet in the corner.
paragraphs.zh: 在接下来的几天里，几乎没有入职引导，他便被卷入了他后来称之为“博尚园区”的诈骗组织运作机器：他被训练去创建假身份、拿到诈骗话术脚本，然后开始夜间作业，每晚手动发出数百条引流式的初次接触消息来诱导新受害者。下班后，他会回到六人间宿舍的上铺休息——这个宿舍比他最初住下的酒店房间稍大一些，拐角处还放着一个厕所。

paragraphs.en: Yet from the very beginning, he says, he was determined to again defy his circumstances. It struck him that he knew more about computers than most of his coworkers, or even his bosses, who seemed to understand only how to use social media, AI tools, and cryptocurrency. Within days, he began daydreaming of using his technical skills to quietly gather information on the compound and, somehow, expose it.
paragraphs.zh: 但他从一开始就说，自己决心再次反抗命运。此后他发现自己比大多数同事，甚至比老板都更懂电脑，老板们似乎只会使用社交媒体、AI 工具和加密货币。几天后，他就开始幻想，是否能利用自己的技术能力悄悄收集该园区的信息，并以某种方式将其曝光。

paragraphs.en: There was, Red Bull came to believe, little to prevent him from leaking the compound’s secrets. Team leaders took employees’ personal phones and put them in a box when they began their shifts, and they were strictly prohibited from taking work devices out of the office. But otherwise, the surveillance of staffers and their own phones seemed surprisingly loose.
paragraphs.zh: 红牛后来意识到，几乎没有什么能真正阻止他把这座“园区”的秘密泄露出去。主管们会在员工上班前把他们的私人手机收走，放进一个箱子，并且严令禁止把工作手机带出办公室。但除此之外，管理层对员工及其手机的监控反而出乎意料地松散。

paragraphs.en: Bosses seemed to depend largely on the fear and despair of Red Bull’s fellow trafficking victims—most of whom had, it seemed to him, lost all hope of resistance. “They tell themselves survival is the only goal, and they shut down anything that feels human,” Red Bull wrote to me. “Empathy, guilt, even memories of who they were before.”
paragraphs.zh: 老板们似乎主要依赖的是红牛同样被贩运而来的受害者身上的恐惧和绝望——在他看来，他们中的大多数已经对反抗失去了全部希望。”他们告诉自己：活下来就是唯一目标，于是把任何看起来像人类的东西都关掉，”红牛这样写给我。 “同情心、内疚，甚至是他们曾经的自己。”

paragraphs.en: He kept his own hope alive in part with a sense that he was different. “Most people don’t have the skills, or the tools, or even the mental strength to fight from the inside,” he wrote. “I can move through the system. I can observe. I can gather evidence, names, scripts, patterns, connections.”
paragraphs.zh: 他之所以能保住希望，部分原因在于他自己有一种与众不同的认知。”大多数人既没有能力，也没有工具，甚至没有足够的心理强度去在内部发起对抗，”他写道。“而我可以在这个系统里穿行，可以观察，可以搜集证据、姓名、话术、套路、关系链。”

paragraphs.en: At times, though, I still struggled to understand what had given Red Bull the conviction to reach out to me, to risk his life rather than merely serve out his time. “Maybe it’s justice, or maybe it’s conscience,” he responded. “If there’s a God, I hope he sees what I’m doing. If there isn’t, then at least I’ll know I stayed human in a place that tries to turn people into monsters.”
paragraphs.zh: 不过有时，我仍然不明白是什么给了红牛那种决心：让他联系我，而不是选择忍受时间过去。“也许是正义，也许是良知，”他回答道。“如果有上帝，我希望他能看到我正在做的事。如果没有，那么至少在一个试图把人变成怪物的地方，我还能知道自己仍是人。”

paragraphs.en: As time passed and the collection of materials Red Bull sent to me mounted, I was also getting the sense that the walls were closing in on him. One day, Red Bull told me, his team leader Amani asked him with menacing calm why he was spending so much time outside—referring, most likely, to the walks when Red Bull would talk to me on Signal—and generating so few new “clients.” Maybe, Amani suggested, a beating or some electric shocks would increase his productivity.
paragraphs.zh: 随着时间推移，我收到的“红牛”提供的材料越来越多，我也越来越感到他的周围形势在收紧。一天，“红牛”告诉我，他的组长阿曼尼用一种带着威胁意味的平静语气问他，为什么他要在外面待这么久——这大概指的是“红牛”在 Signal 上和我通话时要他出去散步那段时间——而且他又拿来很少的新“客户”。或许，阿曼尼暗示，打一顿或用电击一下，能提高他的产能。

paragraphs.en: Around the same time, Red Bull told me that new surveillance cameras had been installed in the office, including on the ceiling both behind and in front of his desk. I told him he should immediately stop communicating with me from the office—it was now far too risky. My editors came to a more severe conclusion: I should shut down my reporting with Red Bull altogether until he was free.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，“红牛”还告诉我，他们在办公室新装了监控摄像头，包括他桌子前后天花板位置。于是我告诉他，必须立刻停止在办公室里和我联系——现在风险太大了。我的编辑们得出的结论更为严厉：在他获得自由之前，我应当完全停止与“红牛”的报道合作。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull had, by this point, sent me a collection of 25 scam scripts and guides in English and Chinese. The documents displayed the anatomy of scamming at a level of resolution I’d never seen before: lists of conversation starters; tutorials on what to do when a target asks for a video call and how to delay until a deepfake model is ready to speak with them; tips on how to complain about overcautious financial institutions so victims don’t get spooked by their own bank’s warnings.
paragraphs.zh: 到那时，“红牛”已经给我提供了25份中英文诈骗脚本和操作手册。那些文件展示了我前所未见的、细致到解剖级别的诈骗流程：开场白清单；当受害人要求视频通话时该如何应对，以及如何拖延时间直到换脸模型（deepfake 模型）准备好与其交谈；以及如何对“过于谨慎的金融机构”提出投诉式说辞，让受害人别被自己银行的预警吓到。

paragraphs.en: Maybe what he’d given me was already enough. Following my editors’ lead, I told Red Bull that it was time to stop. “OK, done,” he said, with his usual quick pivot.
paragraphs.zh: 也许他交给我的内容已经足够。按编辑的建议，我告诉“红牛”，是时候停下来了。“好，明白了，”他用一贯那种反应极快的方式说，“我们停。”

paragraphs.en: A video secretly recorded over a Signal call shows the inside of the Boshang compound’s cafeteria. Red Bull says the food tasted of strange chemicals. Access to the cafeteria was frequently denied to workers for violations as basic as showing up late to a shift or not being in their dorm rooms at check-in time.
paragraphs.zh: 一段在 Signal 通话中秘密录下的视频显示了博上园区餐厅的内部。Red Bull 说，食物尝起来有奇怪的化学味。员工进出餐厅的权限常常被拒，理由甚至是一些最基本的“违规”，比如上班迟到，或在点名报到时不在宿舍里。

paragraphs.en: Now, I told him, he should focus on completing the remaining six months of his contract as safely as possible, and we’d talk again when he was free. But Red Bull, once again, was already several thoughts ahead. If our reporting process was finished, he told me, he wanted to leave now.
paragraphs.zh: 那时，我对他说，他应该先尽量安全地把剩下六个月的合同完成，我们等他脱身后再继续谈。但 Red Bull 再次领先了我几步。如果我们把采访过程收尾，他当时对我说，他想立刻离开。

paragraphs.en: He told me about a plan he’d been concocting to get home: He’d forge an Indian police letter stating that he was under investigation back in Jammu and Kashmir. If he didn’t go back, he’d tell his supervisor, it would cause serious trouble for him, his family, and ultimately the compound. He would plead to take a two-week trip home, deal with the situation, and return. Maybe, he said, his bosses would buy this story and let him leave.
paragraphs.zh: 他告诉我，他已经想好了一个回家的计划：伪造一封印度警方来函，写明他在查谅解拉尔—查谟和克什米尔正受到调查。如果他不回去，他会告诉监工，这会给他本人、他的家人，最终也会给这个园区带来严重麻烦。他会请求请一个两周的回家假，处理完后再回来。也许，他说，老板会相信这个说法，让他离开。

paragraphs.en: I didn’t think it would work, and I told him as much: I warned that his overseers might detect the forgery and punish him. But after all the risky schemes I had already talked Red Bull out of, he seemed more stubbornly intent on this one. I asked him to please wait, and I told him I’d try to find him someone in the region who was more familiar with scam compound escape tactics. I was in touch, for instance, with a Southeast Asian activist who asked to be identified only as “W,” who had experience helping political refugees escape from the region.
paragraphs.zh: 我认为这个办法不太可能成功，也直说了我的判断：我提醒他，主管很可能会识破假信并严惩他。但在我已经把 Red Bull 说退了许多冒险方案之后，他对这次却表现得更固执。于是我请他先等等，并告诉他我会尽量在当地帮他找一个更熟悉诈骗园区逃脱手段的人帮忙。比如，我之前联系过一位东南亚活动人士，只以“W”为名，曾有帮助政治难民逃离该地区的经验。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull suddenly switched into cover story mode as he entered the office lobby. “No problem uncle, you stay relaxed,” he said as he walked by the security guard. “Things will be better soon, OK?” Then he ended the call.
paragraphs.zh: “Red Bull”刚走进办公室大堂，就突然转入了“掩护性”说法。”没问题，叔叔，你先别紧张，”他一边从保安身边走过一边说，“很快就会好起来的，好吗？”然后他结束了通话。

paragraphs.en: At another point in those daily conversations, Red Bull brought up another potential path to freedom: If only he could pay the equivalent of $3,400, he could buy out his contract and go home. He just needed to get the money somehow.
paragraphs.zh: 在那段日常谈话中的另一个时刻，Red Bull又提到了一条可能的自由之路：如果他能凑齐3,400美元的等值金额，他就可以买断合同回家。他只需要想办法弄到这笔钱。

paragraphs.en: In a matter of seconds, a fleet of thoughts ran through my mind. First, a flash of hope for Red Bull and a desire to pay off his debt. Then the realization that, of course, WIRED couldn’t possibly give money to a source in this way, much less reward an organized crime group for human trafficking. The idea violated journalistic ethics—payments to sources are generally considered a corrupting conflict of interest—and would set an unforgivable precedent. I said as much to Red Bull, and he quickly responded that he understood “completely” and that he had never asked me or WIRED to pay.
paragraphs.zh: 我脑海里转念的时间甚至不及几秒。第一时间是对 Red Bull 的一线希望，以及想替他还清债务的冲动。然后我意识到，WIRED不可能以这种方式给消息源送钱，更不用说给一个有组织犯罪团伙在拐卖人口方面“打赏”了。这个想法违反了新闻伦理——向消息源付钱通常被视为会引发利益冲突的腐化行为——而且会树立一种无法原谅的先例。我向 Red Bull 这么说了，他很快回应说他“完全理解”，并且他从未要求我或 WIRED给钱。

paragraphs.en: Even so, the mere mention of that payment option also planted in my mind a different, darker thought that I now couldn’t shake: What if Red Bull was scamming me? I had set aside my initial skepticism of Red Bull once I’d seen enough proof that he was who he said he was: a real person trapped in a grim compound in Laos. Now, almost two weeks into our relationship, the troubling possibility nagged at me: What if he truly was a scam compound insider, but this had been the scam all along? The mere thought felt like a betrayal of all the trust he seemed to have placed in me.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，仅仅提到这个付款选项就在我脑中种下了另一种更阴暗的念头，这个念头我现在根本甩不掉：如果 Red Bull 在骗我呢？当我已看到足够证据确信他真的是他自称的人时，我早已放下了起初对他真实性的怀疑——那是一个被困在老挝阴冷围困营地里的真实人。如今，在我们走到几乎两周关系之后，这个令人不安的可能性一直在困扰我：如果他真的是诈骗园区的内线，但这一切一开始就只是个骗局呢？这种想法让我觉得自己背叛了他似乎已放在我身上的全部信任。

paragraphs.en: I decided to compartmentalize my suspicions, keeping the possibility of an ulterior motive in my mind next to the more likely probability that his intentions were genuine.
paragraphs.zh: 我决定把怀疑心理分开处理，一方面保留他可能另有居心的可能性，另一方面也保持他是真心的这一更高概率。

paragraphs.en: A couple of days later, meanwhile, he mentioned his forged document idea again, and I again suggested he wait for help from someone like W and not risk the scheme. But with every day, I could tell that he was increasingly set on his plan. “I have no other options,” he said. “Let’s see what happens.”
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，几天后，他又提起了伪造文件的想法，我又建议他等着向W那样的人求助，不要把这套计划冒险去做。但我能看得出，他越来越坚定要按自己的方案去做。"我别无选择，"他说，"先看看会怎样。"

paragraphs.en: Just a few days later, on a Saturday afternoon, I was surprised to get an email from the same Proton Mail account that Red Bull had first reached out from but hadn’t used since we’d switched to Signal. Just like that first email, it had no subject line.
paragraphs.zh: 又过了几天，周六下午，我很意外地收到了同一个Proton Mail账户的电子邮件——这就是Red Bull最初联系我的账户，后来我们切换到Signal后就再没用过了。就像那封第一封邮件一样，这封也没有写主题。

paragraphs.en: I opened the message and my mind instantly went blank with dread.
paragraphs.zh: 我打开邮件，瞬间整个人被一阵恐惧冻结，脑子一下子空了。

paragraphs.en: “they people cath me and now they get my phone everything,” it read. “they beaten me andn ow may be they kill me”
paragraphs.zh: “他们抓住了我，现在把我的电话都拿走了，”这句话写道。“他们打了我，现在也许他们会杀了我。”

paragraphs.en: Red Bull had tried his forged-police-document deception. Now, it seemed, the worst possible outcome had come to pass.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 之前用过他伪造警察证件的伎俩。如今，最坏的结果似乎成真了。

paragraphs.en: I suppressed panic as my mind spun through options. I texted my editors and W, in the hopes they might have some idea of how to help. Fifteen minutes after that first email came another, more coherent message from Red Bull: “I’m trapped. I have no way to get out. They have my personal phone and my ID card,” this one read. “If there’s anything you can do, please help me.”
paragraphs.zh: 我在脑中飞速思考各种可能性时按捺住了恐慌。我给编辑和W发了短信，希望他们能想出办法帮忙。第一封邮件发出后十五分钟，又收到一封更清楚的来信，出自Red Bull之手： “我被困住了。我没法出去。他们拿走了我的个人手机和身份证。” 他写道：“如果你们能做点什么，请帮帮我。”

paragraphs.en: In the meantime, W responded to me on Signal. Over the phone, we hurriedly talked through what we could possibly do to increase Red Bull’s chances of survival. I didn’t know how Red Bull was emailing me, but W cautioned that it would be dangerous to respond. His bosses knew he’d lied to them to try to escape. It still seemed they didn’t know he was talking to a journalist and leaking their secrets.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，W通过Signal回了我信息。我们在电话里匆忙商量，尽可能提高Red Bull生存下来的机会。我不知道他是怎么发邮件给我的，但W提醒我，回复会很危险。他的上级已经知道他为了逃脱而对他们说谎了。看起来他们还不知道他在和记者联系、泄露他们的罪行。

paragraphs.en: If they found out, there was little doubt they would kill him. “Brutally,” W said. “There’s no way he’d get out of this area alive.” He advised that I wait to hear more from Red Bull about his situation and how to safely communicate.
paragraphs.zh: “如果他们发现了，他肯定会杀了他。‘会被残忍对待。’W说。‘他几乎不可能活着离开这里。’他建议我先等等，看Red Bull后面会怎么说，尤其是关于他的处境和如何安全联络的事。

paragraphs.en: Twenty-four excruciating hours passed before I received another email from Red Bull—a long, stream-of-consciousness block of frantic text.
paragraphs.zh: 又过去了二十四个煎熬的小时，我才收到Red Bull的下一封邮件——一大段意识流般、慌乱又杂乱的文字。

paragraphs.en: “Last night those people beat me I am still hungry I have not eaten anything they disconnected my card my personal phone and everything today they will decide what to do with me the Indian team leader and everyone sat in front of me and said do you know who we are and they beat me again and then made me sit back in the office today I have to accept that whatever I did was fake and I have to accept my mistake I cannot run away from here I have no money and I cannot even go outside the gate I am contacting you from the system PC if you have any way then send me an email I will check it and tell W to talk to me on my email and those people are telling me to give them 20k yuan they said if I give it they will leave me without doing anything more please tell W to reply to my email whenever they torture me and bring me back to the office I am only on the system PC have a nice evening ahead”
paragraphs.zh: “昨晚他们打了我，我还在饿着肚子，今天一整天都没吃东西；他们把我的卡和我个人手机都断开了联系。今天他们会决定对我做什么。那个印度团队负责人和其他人坐在我面前，问我‘你知道我们是谁吗？’然后又把我打了一顿，之后让我回到办公室。今天我必须承认自己之前做的一切都是假的，必须承认自己的错误。我不能从这里逃走，我没有钱，甚至连大门外都走不了。我正用系统电脑给你发消息，如果你有什么办法就发邮件，我会看，并告诉W让他用我的邮箱联系我。那些人让我给他们20,000元人民币，说我只要给，他们就会放我走，不再继续折腾。请告诉W无论何时都回复我的邮件——每次他们折磨我再把我带回办公室时，我都只能用这台系统电脑。祝你晚安。”

paragraphs.en: Before I could respond to this email, I got a Signal message: “Red.”
paragraphs.zh: 在我还没来得及回复这封邮件时，我收到了一条 Signal 消息：“Red。”

paragraphs.en: “Bull,” I wrote back.
paragraphs.zh: “Bull。”我回道。

paragraphs.en: He wrote quickly, this time with the short version: He’d been put in a room and told again to find someone to pay 20,000 yuan for his release, the equivalent of around $2,800.
paragraphs.zh: 这次他写得很快，只说了简短内容：他们把他关进一个房间，再次要求他找人出钱20,000元来换取释放，约合2,800美元。

paragraphs.en: In the midst of this life-or-death crisis, I couldn’t help but think that this might just as easily be the endgame of the scam I’d suspected in the back of my mind: Hook a journalist’s attention, lure him in, give him responsibility for a source’s safety, and now require a payment to save his life.
paragraphs.zh: 在这场生死危机关头，我忍不住想，这很可能正是我心里早有猜测的骗局终局：先吸引一名记者注意、把他套进去、让他背负起保护消息源安全的责任，然后再要求付款来换取生命。

paragraphs.en: Regardless, my editors had made clear to me that neither WIRED nor I could pay Red Bull or his captors. They were, in fact, warier than ever that he might be scamming me. But the more likely truth, I still felt, was that this nightmare was all entirely real.
paragraphs.zh: 不管怎样，我的编辑早已明确告诉我，WIRED和我都不能支付Red Bull或他的绑架者。事实上，他们比以往任何时候都更警惕他可能在对我诈骗。但我仍然感觉，更可能的真相是：这场噩梦绝对完全真实。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull seemed to have his phone back—likely to allow him to find someone to pay his ransom—but it felt too risky to call him. I texted him, suggesting he instead try to speak to W about who might be able to help him escape. W was far more experienced in these situations—and if Red Bull were monitored, he’d at least be caught speaking to an activist rather than a reporter.
paragraphs.zh: 红牛似乎拿回了手机——很可能是为了让他找到人去给他付赎金——但给他打电话太冒险了。我给他发了条短信，建议他改为找W谈谈，看看谁能帮他逃出去。W在这类情况下更有经验，而且如果红牛在被监控，至少他是在和一名活动人士交谈，而不是记者。

paragraphs.en: I also told Red Bull that, as terrible as I felt that he was going through this hell, I wouldn’t be able to pay his ransom, any more than I’d been able to pay out his contract.
paragraphs.zh: 我还告诉红牛，虽然我很痛心他正在经历这种噩梦般的折磨，但我也不能给他付赎金，就像我当初不能替他付合同时一样。

paragraphs.en: “Okay,” Red Bull wrote. “I understand.” He asked me to tell W to get in touch, and I told him I would.
paragraphs.zh: “好，”红牛写道，“我明白了。”他让我转告W联系他，我回答说我会去做。

paragraphs.en: I watched as he set Signal’s disappearing message feature to delete messages after only five seconds, a sign of how closely he feared he was being watched.
paragraphs.zh: 我看到他把 Signal 的“消息自动销毁”功能设成仅保留五秒，即便如此，也看得出他有多么害怕自己正在被人监视。

paragraphs.en: He posted a thumbs-up emoji. Then it was gone.
paragraphs.zh: 他发了一个“点赞”表情。然后它就消失了。

paragraphs.en: Over the next few days, I spoke with one person after another who I hoped might be able to help Red Bull, perhaps even by paying his ransom: Erin West, W, W’s boss at the nonprofit he worked for. One by one, each of them backed away—either from concerns about rewarding a scam compound’s human trafficking, suspicion that Red Bull’s story might itself be a scam, or some combination of the two.
paragraphs.zh: 在接下来的几天里，我先后与一个又一个人交谈，希望他们能帮Red Bull，甚至出钱支付赎金：Erin West，W在其所在非营利机构的上司。每个人都一个接一个地退了出来——要么是出于不愿鼓励一个专门从事诈骗集团的人口贩卖罪行，或是担心Red Bull的故事本身可能也是骗局，或是两者兼有。

paragraphs.en: West, despite her enormous enthusiasm when Red Bull first came forward, now said it sounded like a human trafficking racket she’d heard of elsewhere, in which fake victims solicit fake ransoms. W got as far as speaking to Red Bull on multiple Signal voice calls but was overwhelmed by his panicked energy and thought his desperate pleas for the ransom payment—and promises to pay W back in the future—were dubious. “It sounded like ‘Send me one bitcoin and I’ll send you two,’” W told me afterward.
paragraphs.zh: West在Red Bull最初站出来时虽热情很高，但后来她表示，这听起来像是她在其他地方听说过的人口贩卖诈骗团伙：伪装的受害者在虚构假人质，向外索取假赎金。W与Red Bull进行过多次Signal语音通话，但被他带着恐慌的情绪淹没，并认为他急切请求支付赎金——还附带承诺未来会还钱——的说法令人怀疑。W事后对我说：“听起来就像‘你给我一枚比特币，我就给你两枚’。”

paragraphs.en: But I still felt I owed it to Red Bull to take his situation at face value and—assuming it was all true—do whatever I could within the bounds of journalistic ethics to get him out.
paragraphs.zh: 但我仍然觉得我有责任把Red Bull的处境按其字面陈述来对待，并且在假设一切属实的前提下，在新闻伦理范围内尽我所能帮助他脱困。

paragraphs.en: Three days had passed since he was first held ransom. It was becoming clearer that he was no longer being closely monitored, perhaps because his captors were growing bored with him. I decided to risk a call. “Things are not going good,” he told me with typical understatement, speaking softly, close to the phone’s microphone. He told me he had a fever, that he’d been beaten several times, slapped and kicked and made to confess that he’d forged the Indian police document. On one occasion the bosses put a white powder into a cup of water and told him to drink it. He found that it made him unnaturally talkative and confident but then gave him a rash of raised red bumps on his skin. He was sometimes sent back to his dormitory to sleep, he told me, but hadn’t eaten in days and was deprived of water for long stretches.
paragraphs.zh: 自从他第一次被拿去赎人身以来，已经过去三天了。越来越明显的是，他似乎不再被人严密监视了，也许是因为他的绑匪对他已经感到厌倦。于是我决定冒险给他打电话。“情况不太好，”他说，仍然一贯地用很轻的语气低声说着，声音贴着手机麦克风。​他告诉我自己发了高烧，已经被打了好几次，被扇耳光和踢打，并被逼着承认他伪造了印度警方文件。有一次，老板让人往一杯水里倒入白色粉末，叫他喝。他发现那让他变得异常健谈和自信，但随后全身又起了大片红色丘疹。谈话中他又说，有时他们会把他送回宿舍让他睡觉，但他已经几天没吃饭，且长时间被断水。

paragraphs.en: He’d written to various Indian embassies and consulates across Southeast Asia, but none had responded. “No one is going to help me. I don’t know why,” he said a few minutes into our call, his voice finally breaking into a muffled sob, the first time I’d ever heard him express self-pity.
paragraphs.zh: 他向东南亚多地的印度使馆和领事馆写了信，但都没有人回应。“没有人会来救我，我也不明白为什么，”在我们通话几分钟后，他这样说着，声音终于哽咽，第一次听见他流露出自怜。

paragraphs.en: Then he quickly controlled himself with a single breath. “I want to cry,” he said. “But let’s see.”
paragraphs.zh: 接着他用一口气很快把自己镇住。“我想哭，”他说。“但先等等。”

paragraphs.en: Four days after he’d first been caught trying to escape and held for ransom, Red Bull texted me to say that something had changed in the compound. Everything was strangely quiet, and no one had summoned him to the office. When he asked some of his coworkers, they told him there were rumors the Laotian police were planning a raid. Their Chinese bosses had gotten a tip from someone on the inside and were laying low.
paragraphs.zh: 在他第一次试图逃跑并被扣留赎金后四天，Red Bull 给我发短信说园区里出现了变化。一切都异常安静，没有人去叫他去办公室。后来他问了几个同事，他们告诉他有传闻说老挝警方正在计划一次突击。他的中国老板似乎得到了内线消息，所以暂时低调起来了。

paragraphs.en: The next day, with rumors of a raid still circulating through the compound, Red Bull got a hopeful message from the Indian embassy in Laos. “Please share your passport copy, company ID,” it read. “Embassy will take necessary action to rescue you.”
paragraphs.zh: 第二天，尽管对这片园区里是否会发生突击搜查的传闻仍在流传，Red Bull却收到了来自老挝印度大使馆的一则带着希望的信息。消息写着：“请提供你的护照复印件、公司身份证。” “大使馆将采取必要行动来营救你。”

paragraphs.en: Salvation seemed to be on the horizon. But then more days passed, and—nothing. The embassy stopped responding to Red Bull’s messages. Late one night, I managed to get an Indian embassy official on the phone after several tries. He seemed confused about which person we were talking about, then repeated the government’s vague assurance that it would rescue him, and hung up.
paragraphs.zh: 救援似乎就在眼前。可随后又过了几天，结果——没有任何进展。大使馆停止回复 Red Bull 的消息。一天深夜，我在多次尝试后终于让一名印度大使馆官员接起了电话。他似乎搞不清我们在说的是哪一个人，随后反复重复了政府那种模糊的保证：会去营救他，然后挂断了电话。

paragraphs.en: As the days went by—with no more clarity from the Indian government, no police raid, and no one willing to pay for his freedom—Red Bull seemed to be sinking into fatalism. One day I woke up to a series of messages offering up a confession, as if he feared he might die in the room where he was being held and wanted to absolve himself of sin.
paragraphs.zh: 随着时间一天天过去——印度政府没有给出更多清晰说明，没有警察突击搜查，也没有人愿意出钱换回他的自由——Red Bull似乎开始陷入一种走向死亡的悲观情绪。有一天，我醒来时看到一连串消息，像是在递交一份忏悔书，似乎他担心自己可能在被囚的房间里死去，于是想先为自己洗清“罪责”。

paragraphs.en: “I want to say something honestly. When I first talked to you I said I never scammed anyone. That was not fully true,” he wrote. “The truth is the Chinese bosses forced me to bring two people into the scam. I did not do it by choice. I feel guilty about it every day. That is why I want to tell the full truth now.”
paragraphs.zh: “我想说一件真心话。第一次和你谈时，我说过自己从未诈骗过任何人。那并不完全真实，”他写道。“事实上，是中国老板们逼我带进来两个人参与诈骗。我不是出于自愿那样做的。我每天都因此感到内疚。也正因为如此，我现在才想把全部真相说出来。”

paragraphs.en: He later told me more details of those two victims. From one, he’d taken $504. From the other, around $11,000. He gave me both of their names. I tried contacting them but couldn’t find one, and the other never responded. For the larger of those two sums, Red Bull should have received a commission based on the scam compound’s incentive structure. But he says he was never paid any reward beyond his meager base salary.
paragraphs.zh: 他后来又告诉了我这两位受害者的更多细节。一个被他骗走了504美元，另一个则被骗走了约11,000美元。他把两个人的名字都告诉了我。我试着联系他们，但一个始终找不到，另一个始终没有回复。按这家诈骗作坊的奖励机制算，在较大那一笔金额中，Red Bull本应拿到一部分提成，但他表示自己除了微薄的底薪外，从未拿到任何报酬。

paragraphs.en: I’d later look back at the picture of the office whiteboard Red Bull had shared early on. On it I could see, quite clearly, the Chinese name the compound had given him, “Machao,” next to the sum of $504. I had entirely missed this, though he’d made no attempt to hide it.
paragraphs.zh: 后来我又回看了Red Bull最早给我看过的办公室白板照片。上面清楚地写着作坊给他起的中文名字“Machao”，旁边是504美元这个数字。我当时完全没注意到，尽管他并没有试图把它掩盖。

paragraphs.en: “I am trusting you with my real story,” Red Bull concluded his confession. “This is the truth.”
paragraphs.zh: “我把我的真实经历都托付给你了，” Red Bull在交代完后说，“这就是事实。”

paragraphs.en: After 10 days in limbo, Red Bull told me that he and his coworkers had been ordered to pack their things. The office computers had been boxed up and stored in the dormitory. The entire staff was moving to a new building a few hundred feet away, and the workers were told they’d have to continue their work from these temporary dorm rooms rather than the office. According to the rumors, a raid was finally coming.
paragraphs.zh: 在搁置了十天后，Red Bull告诉我，他和同事们已经接到打包行李的命令。办公室里的电脑都被装进纸箱，存放到了宿舍里。整个团队要搬到几百英尺外的新楼里，而员工们被告知要从这些临时宿舍里继续工作，而不是回到办公室。据说有风声称，一场突击搜查终于要来了。

paragraphs.en: Throughout this time, Red Bull was treated more or less like a dog, as he described it, a pariah set apart from the other workers: He had no bedding; sometimes he slept on the floor and was fed only when someone remembered to give him food, often spoiled leftovers. He lost weight and suffered from body aches, fever, and what felt like the flu.
paragraphs.zh: 在这段时间里，雷德·布尔几乎被当成狗一样对待，正如他所说，是一个与其他工人隔离开的贱民：他没有床铺可睡；有时只能睡在地板上，而且只有在有人想起给他食物时才有饭吃，而那常常是变质的剩菜。 他瘦了，出现了全身酸痛、发热以及类似流感的症状。

paragraphs.en: Yet somehow, even then, Red Bull was still motivated to keep digging.
paragraphs.zh: 然而不知怎么地，就在那时，雷德·布尔依然有动力继续挖下去。

paragraphs.en: During this hiatus from the office, work devices were now allowed into the dormitory—a loosening of security that Red Bull realized could offer him an opportunity. One day when one of his roommates was asleep, he found the man’s work phone.
paragraphs.zh: 在这次离开办公室的间隙里，工作设备被允许带进宿舍——这一放松的安保让雷德·布尔意识到可能是个机会。一天，当一个室友正在睡觉时，他找到了那人的工作手机。

paragraphs.en: He had seen the man enter his passcode over his shoulder, and now quickly unlocked it. Red Bull then connected his own personal phone to the man’s WhatsApp using the app’s “linked device” feature, allowing him to read the scam compound’s internal messaging. He used that access to make screen recordings, meticulously scrolling through months of the compound’s internal conversations, as well as all the screenshots of chat logs with victims his colleagues had posted.
paragraphs.zh: 他曾看到那人从肩膀后面输入手机密码，于是很快把它解锁了。之后，雷德·布尔用自己的个人手机，通过应用的“已连接设备”功能连接上那人的 WhatsApp，让他能读取该诈骗仓库里的内部消息。他利用这次访问进行了屏幕录制，仔细浏览了数月来的内部聊天记录，以及同事发布的所有与受害者聊天截图。

paragraphs.en: Another day, he found his own work phone left unattended in a different dorm room—he hadn’t had access to it since he was first caught trying to escape—and repeated the WhatsApp linking trick so that he could access that device’s messages, too, from his personal phone. Then he made another screen recording of scrolling through its chats. Together, the videos added up to a detailed record of three months of the compound’s day-to-day operations. Red Bull sent me samples of these recordings, but the full videos ran to nearly 10 gigabytes, far more than he could text me from his phone’s data plan.
paragraphs.zh: 又过了一天，他发现自己的工作手机忘在了另一个宿舍房间里——自从他第一次试图逃跑后就一直拿不到这部手机，于是又重复了一遍 WhatsApp 的绑定技巧，让自己也能用个人手机查看这台设备里的消息。随后，他又录制了一段滚动浏览聊天记录的屏幕录屏。这样一来，这些视频合在一起就形成了该园区三个月日常运作的详细记录。Red Bull 把这些录音样本发给了我，但完整视频有近 10GB，远远超出他从手机套餐里发消息给我所能承受的流量。

paragraphs.en: A week later, after he and his coworkers had moved to the new building, Red Bull sent me a very different, more dramatic series of short clips: One showed dozens of South Asian men standing outside of a high-rise building, being lined up by what appeared to be Laotian police in khaki and black uniforms. Another showed a similar-looking crowd sitting in rows in a lobby. The raids, Red Bull told me, had arrived, sweeping the scam operations that hadn’t taken the precaution of vacating the old building, as his bosses had. Now these videos were circulating among workers who had only narrowly missed the crackdown.
paragraphs.zh: 一周后，在他和同事搬到新楼后，Red Bull 给我发来了一组截然不同、更加戏剧化的短视频。第一段里，几十名南亚男子站在一栋高层楼外，被穿着卡其色和黑色制服、看起来像老挝警察的人把他们排成一排。另一段则显示一群人外貌相似的人在大厅里分排坐着。Red Bull 告诉我，突击行动已经发生，那些像他的老板那样提前迁走旧楼的人避开了，仍在里面作案的诈骗行动都被一并扫荡。这些视频随后就在那些差点在这次清剿中躲过去的工人之间流传。

paragraphs.en: As the rest of the compound’s operations struggled to adapt to their new makeshift workspace, Red Bull, of course, had already been stuck in purgatory for weeks. He pleaded with his bosses to be released, arguing that he was no use to them. He had no money, and clearly there was no one willing to pay his ransom. He was dead weight, taking up space when they were already crowded into their temporary building.
paragraphs.zh: 当这处园区的其他部门还在努力适应新的临时作业点时，Red Bull 当然也已被困在类似煎熬中整整数周。于是他恳求老板放人，理由是自己对他们已经没了用处。他身无分文，而且显然没有人愿意替他支付赎金。他成了累赘，在他们已经挤满临时楼栋的情况下只占用空间。

paragraphs.en: Shockingly, his bosses agreed. Rather than kill him, they told him he could go.
paragraphs.zh: 令人震惊的是，他的老板居然同意了。老板告诉他，不杀他，并准许他离开。

paragraphs.en: To scrape together enough money for a return journey, Red Bull borrowed a few hundred dollars from his brother. Then he wrote to an Indian acquaintance who had a position in a scam compound nearby, telling him that he needed to go home to see his family but would soon return. If the acquaintance could send enough money to buy plane tickets, Red Bull proposed, he’d let the man take the recruitment fee when he returned. Soon he had several hundred more dollars in his account. Red Bull had scammed a scammer, and he’d found a path home.
paragraphs.zh: 为了凑够返程的钱，Red Bull向哥哥借了几百美元。随后他给一位住在附近诈骗园区、在当地有职位的印度熟人写信，说自己得回去看家人，但很快就会回来。如果那位熟人能寄足够的钱买机票，Red Bull提出，等他回来后可以把招募费交给对方。很快，他账户里又多了几百美元。Red Bull骗了一个骗子，也就找到了回家的路。

paragraphs.en: In late July, Red Bull’s team leader, Amani, intercepted him outside the dormitory, handed him his passport, and told him it was time to leave. Red Bull explained that most of his things, including his shoes, were in his room. He was wearing only a pair of flip-flops.
paragraphs.zh: 7月底，Red Bull的队长Amani在宿舍外拦下了他，交还了他的护照，并告诉他该离开了。Red Bull解释说，自己大部分东西，包括鞋子，都还在屋里。他身上只穿着一双人字拖。

paragraphs.en: Amani told him he didn’t care. 50k himself was waiting in an Audi to drive Red Bull to the border of the Golden Triangle region. From there, he’d be on his own. He got in the back of the car in his flip-flops and left.
paragraphs.zh: Amani对这些说法不在乎。名叫50k的人坐在一辆奥迪车里等着，把他送到“金三角”地区边境。从那里开始，Red Bull就只能靠自己了。他穿着人字拖坐上后排并离开了。

paragraphs.en: Later, when Red Bull had finally escaped, he would marvel at this last slight, as if it were somehow worse than all the slaps, kicks, drugging, and starvation he’d endured. “I never expected this from them,” he wrote to me, punctuating his text with crying emojis. “They didn’t even allow me to wear my shoes.”
paragraphs.zh: 后来，当Red Bull终于逃脱时，他会认为这最后的小动作比他曾经历过的所有耳光、踢打、下药和挨饿还更伤人。“我从没想到他们会这样对我，”他给我发短信时写道，消息中还夹着哭泣表情符号。“他们甚至不让我穿鞋。”

paragraphs.en: A few days after that drive to the border, near the end of a journey that involved buses, a train, and a dirt-cheap plane itinerary with no fewer than five layovers, Red Bull was finally back in India. During a stopover on the way to his home village, he began sending me the WhatsApp screen-recording videos he’d smuggled out of the compound on his phone.
paragraphs.zh: 在那次前往边境的行程结束后的几天里，这段旅程先后乘坐了大巴、火车，再加上一趟廉价航班（中间至少有五次经停），Red Bull 终于回到了印度。前往家乡村庄的途中，在一次中转时，他开始向我发送他偷偷藏在手机里、从园区带出来的 WhatsApp 屏幕录屏视频。

paragraphs.en: For an analysis of the thousands of pages of materials that Red Bull leaked to WIRED, click here.
paragraphs.zh: 关于 Red Bull 泄露给 WIRED 的数千页材料分析，请点击此处。

paragraphs.en: These files would turn out to be the most significant and unique material he provided me. A team of reporters at WIRED would later convert them into a 4,200-page PDF of screenshots and share them with scam compound experts. The document, we’d discover, offers a detailed diary of life inside the compound, cataloging every successful scam it achieved during those months and laying out the scale and hierarchy of the operation. It also reveals the mundane minute-by-minute life of the forced laborers carrying out those scams, from their daily schedules to the fines and punishments they received to the Orwellian language their bosses used to manipulate, cajole, and discipline them—some of which is included in the text interspersed throughout this story.
paragraphs.zh: 这些文件后来被证明是他交给我的最重要、也最独特的材料。WIRED 的一支记者团队随后将其整理为一份 4,200 页的截图 PDF，并与反诈骗园区专家共享。后来我们发现，这份文档详细记录了他在园区里的生活日记，列出了那几个月里它成功实施的每一桩诈骗，并勾勒出该行动的规模和组织层级。它还呈现了那些被迫劳工在执行诈骗时琐碎而又按分钟分割的日常生活，从每日作息到他们所受的罚款和惩罚，再到上级用来操控、唆使和惩戒他们的“奥威尔式”语言——其中一些内容已穿插在本文中。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, no one had given Red Bull the help he needed to escape—not the human rights groups I tried to connect him with, not the Indian government (which never rescued him as promised), and not WIRED. Red Bull had rescued himself. Now, despite that complete lack of incentive or aid, amid the most desperate circumstances, he had obtained and given me the biggest data prize yet.
paragraphs.zh: 最终，没有人给 Red Bull 提供他所需的逃生帮助——我尝试对接的人权组织没有，印度政府也没有（它从未按承诺去救他），WIRED 亦未能做到。Red Bull 是靠自己把自己救了出来。如今，尽管在最绝望的处境下，他既没有明确奖励也没有外部支持，却仍拿到并交给我迄今为止最大的资料成果。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull, back in his home country of India.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull，当他回到祖国印度时。

paragraphs.en: Red Bull’s hands weren’t clean. He had admitted to me that, under duress, he’d scammed two innocent people. But despite my fears and those of the others I’d tried to connect him with, his motivation to act as a whistleblower had proven to be pure.
paragraphs.zh: Red Bull 的手也并非干净。正如他向我承认的那样，在压力之下，他曾骗过两名无辜的人。但尽管我担心他，他也曾向我证明；尽管我曾试着把他介绍给一些人，他仍然坚持举报的动机是纯粹的。

paragraphs.en: Now there could finally be no doubt: Red Bull was real.
paragraphs.zh: 现在终于没有疑问了：Red Bull 的确存在。

paragraphs.en: On a quiet backstreet of a city somewhere in India, I wait alone—surrounded by several dozen macaque monkeys lounging, grooming each other, and parkouring off the neighborhood’s balconies and electrical wires. Then the monkey troop disperses into the trees and onto rooftops, and a white SUV emerges around a corner, drives up the street, and stops in front of me.
paragraphs.zh: 在印度某个城市的一条静谧小巷里，我独自站着——周围有几十只猕猴懒散地躺着、互相梳理毛发，并在街坊的阳台和电线上来回奔跳嬉闹。然后猴群散开，窜进树冠和屋顶，一个白色 SUV 从转角冒了出来，沿着街道开来，在我面前停下。

paragraphs.en: A door opens, and out steps Red Bull, displaying the same shy smile he had on his face when he picked up my first Signal video call. He looks smaller than I imagined, very thin, but more put together than he did on my phone screen, with a button-up flannel shirt and a fresh haircut. As he walks toward me, he breaks into a bigger, less restrained grin, and I shake his hand.
paragraphs.zh: 一扇门打开了，Red Bull走了出来，他的脸上带着我第一次通过 Signal 视频通话见到他时同样害羞的微笑。他看起来比我想象的更瘦小，但比我在手机屏幕上看到的状态更精神，穿着一件扣子式法兰绒衬衫，头发也剪得清爽。随着他走向我，他露出了更大、也更不那么克制的笑容，我伸手与他握手。

paragraphs.en: Now that he’s finally free, Red Bull has given me permission to reveal his real name: Mohammad Muzahir.
paragraphs.zh: 现在他终于获得自由，Red Bull已经允许我公开他的真名：Mohammad Muzahir。

paragraphs.en: “I’m feeling very, very happy to meet with you. I’ve been waiting a long time to meet with you and share everything, face-to-face,” Muzahir says after we’ve checked him into his hotel and we’re riding in the SUV to mine. “I have no words to express it right now.”
paragraphs.zh: “我非常、非常高兴能见到你。我等了很久，想和你当面聊一切。”在我把他办理好酒店入住后，我们坐上SUV前往我这里，Muzahir说。 “我现在完全说不出该怎么表达我的感受。”

paragraphs.en: The three months between Muzahir’s escape and this in-person meeting have been far from easy. He’s virtually broke, but he can’t bring himself to focus on building websites and Facebook pages as he has done in the past, nor does he even have a laptop. Instead he has worked as a waiter and taken construction jobs to survive. When Muzahir isn’t working or applying to jobs and universities abroad—so far without success—he obsessively researches scam operations on his phone, which is cracked on both front and back, with glitching lines across its damaged screen.
paragraphs.zh: 在Muzahir逃离后的这三个月里，他的生活远非轻松。几乎一贫如洗，但他始终无法再像过去那样专注于搭建网站和运营Facebook主页，也甚至连台式电脑都没有。他只能靠当服务员和做建筑工来维持生计。当Muzahir不在工作，或没有在申请工作和海外大学——到目前为止都没有成功——时，他就会在手机上反复研究诈骗团伙的运作。那台手机前后都裂了，屏幕上满是损坏导致的干扰斑线。

paragraphs.en: In that research, Muzahir has come to believe that almost all of the men rounded up in the raids that displaced his compound were later released back into the Golden Triangle. He assumes the police action was all just for show and barely disrupted the scam operations there. He has also learned that the Boshang operation that indentured him has since relocated to Cambodia, taking many of his former coworkers with it.
paragraphs.zh: 在那次调查中，穆扎希越来越确信，在这次突袭中被抓起来、并将他的园区赶离现场的几乎所有男子，后来都被放回了金三角地区。他认为警方行动更像是做样子，对那里持续的诈骗运作几乎没有实质性打击。穆扎希还得知，把他签了“契约”并囚困其中的博尚（Boshang）集团随后已迁往柬埔寨，并带走了他许多原本的同事。

paragraphs.en: Muzahir sleeps as little as three hours a night, he tells me after we sit down in an empty lounge in the basement of my hotel. He’s haunted, he says, by the fact that the scam compound he escaped, and dozens like it, are still operating and even expanding across lawless zones in Southeast Asia, and now other parts of the world. He thinks compulsively about the colleagues he left behind. He feels crushing guilt, too, about the two people he scammed, even while telling himself it was a necessary precursor to his actions as a whistleblower. He dreams of earning enough to somehow pay the two men back. “Honestly this is not a happy ending to the story,” he says.
paragraphs.zh: 穆扎希告诉我，在我们在我酒店地下室的一间空荡休息室坐下后，他每晚最多只睡三个小时。他说他一直被这样一个事实困扰：他逃离的那个诈骗园区，以及更多同类园区，仍在运作，甚至还在东南亚的法外地带不断扩张，如今又扩到了世界其他地区。他反复想起自己留下的同事，内心非常痛苦。他也为自己诈骗过的两个人感到压抑般的内疚，尽管他反复告诉自己，这只是他成为告密者行为之前不得不迈出的一步。他梦想着赚到足够的钱，设法把那两个人补偿回去。“老实说，这不是个圆满的结局，”他说。

paragraphs.en: After experiencing so many personal betrayals—and working for an operation where industrialized betrayal was, in fact, the business model—Muzahir’s more fundamental problem is that he has trouble putting faith in anyone. He’s reluctant to engage with even the human-rights NGOs and survivor groups I’ve tried to introduce him to. “These people are just wasting time and giving false hope,” he wrote to me at one point. “I’m not trusting too much in people.”
paragraphs.zh: 在经历了如此多的人生背叛——并且长期在一个把“工业化背叛”当作商业模式的机构工作——穆扎希更根本的问题，是他很难再对任何人建立信任。即使是我曾经试图介绍给他的维权 NGO 和幸存者组织，他也不愿意接触。“这些人只是浪费时间，只是在给我假希望，”他曾给我写道。“我对别人是不会过于信任的。”

paragraphs.en: Somehow I’ve become an exception to that near-universal mistrust. But now that we’re finally meeting in person, I feel compelled to confess to Muzahir that there were times when I didn’t trust him—that even when he most needed my help, I still feared, incorrectly, that he might be scamming me.
paragraphs.zh: 不知为何，我成了这几乎普遍不信任中的一个例外。但现在我们终于见面了，我觉得有必要向穆扎希坦白：有些时候我确实不信任他——甚至在他最需要我帮助的时候，我仍然错误地担心，他可能在对我进行诈骗。

paragraphs.en: To my relief, he just grins at this. “You did good,” Muzahir says. If I had paid off his contract or even paid his ransom, he points out, he would have left the compound before he had a chance to record and share the operation’s full WhatsApp conversations.
paragraphs.zh: 令我松了一口气的是，他只是笑了笑。穆扎希尔说：“你做得好。”他指出，如果我替他结清合同款，哪怕支付赎金，他在有机会把作案期间全部 WhatsApp 聊天记录录下并公开之前，就已经离开了这个园区。

paragraphs.en: Muzahir is eager now for WIRED to release our full analysis of that data. I’ve pointed out to him that, when we do, the Chinese mafia could find a way to retaliate against him in India, or elsewhere if he follows through on his plan to leave the country. We could obscure his identity, but his team was small enough that it will likely still be immediately clear to his former bosses who the leaker was—even if we didn’t publish this detailed narrative of his experience.
paragraphs.zh: 穆扎希尔现在急切希望《连线》发布我们对这些数据的完整分析。我已经告诉过他，一旦我们这样做，中国黑帮可能会在印度找到报复他的方式，或者如果他真的按计划离开国家，也可能在其他地方报复他。我们可以模糊他的身份，但他的团队太小了，即使我们不发表这份他经历的详细叙述，也很可能让他的前上级立刻认出是他泄密。

paragraphs.en: Muzahir responds that he’s willing to accept that risk to get his story out—including his real identity. After everything he has suffered, Muzahir is still idealistic enough to hope that his experience will serve not only as a warning but as a source of inspiration to others like him.
paragraphs.zh: 穆扎希尔回应说，他愿意为了把自己的故事说出来——包括真实姓名——而接受这种风险。经历了这一切后，穆扎希尔仍然足够理想主义，仍希望自己的经历不仅能作为一次警示，也能成为像他一样的人们的灵感来源。

paragraphs.en: As he explains that decision, I can see more clearly than ever before the motivation driving every risk he has taken: He’s speaking not only to me but to every potential resister or whistleblower inside the burgeoning scam compound industry and the global power structures that enable it, to its survivors, to the hundreds of thousands of other voiceless people trapped in its systems of modern slavery.
paragraphs.zh: 当他阐述这个决定时，我比以往更清楚地看到驱动他每一次冒险的动机：他发声的不只是对我，而是对日益壮大的诈骗园区行业里每一个可能的反抗者或举报人，对促成这一行业的全球权力结构，对其幸存者，对被困在其现代奴役体系中的、数十万名其他无声者。

paragraphs.en: “When someone reads about me, then maybe a lot of Red Bulls will stand up and speak,” Muzahir says with his usual shy smile. “When a lot of Red Bulls speak in this world, it will help to make things better.”
paragraphs.zh: “当有人读到我的故事时，也许就会有更多红牛站出来发声。”穆扎希尔带着一贯害羞的笑容说。“当更多红牛在这个世界上发声时，会有助于让事情变得更好。”
